boys working with him to see that

he got home, had been little ahead

Excitement was created In the
Spokane valley just east of the city
limits yesterday noon by a prairie
fire that broke out near Subway,
caused by spark from an engine
on the N. P. 1t took a hard fight
by the residents to get the flames
checked.


Lord ShoIto Douglas, son Of the
Marquis of Queensbury of prize
ring fame, has been in Spokane
looking for his wife, a former va
riety actress, who deserted him.
Lord Sholto was broke and while
here shipped with a Canadian sur
Vey1ng crew.


Attorney n. m. Stephens will
leave for Chicago Friday to appear
before the Interstate Commerce
commission in a further hearing of
the Spokane rate case on July II.
An effort will be made at that time
to have the commission cause the
rates to become effective as soon
as possible. Members of the rate
committee of the chamber of COM
merce believe that all of the local
railroads will soon come to the new
rates.


Judge Huneke has ordered that
the custody of the young son of
David S. and Pearl Beekley be Civ
en- to the father during the pen
dency of the divorce hearing. Prior
to this time restraining order has
been issued against the husband
forbidding him to even see the
child. Affidavits filed by people
acquainted with the parties caused
the court to change it's ruling.
